comment: Clinical comparator anchors for side-effect / cost / cumulative-burden framing in comp-036. Not used directly in the receptor-occupancy model; used in the comparator framing section of the result.

anakinra_sc_qd_for_gout_flare.regimen: 100 mg SC once daily x 3-5 days (off-label) or 100 mg SC once daily x 5 days (Saag 2021 RCT)
anakinra_sc_qd_for_gout_flare.validated_clinical_trial: Saag KG et al. A Randomized, Phase II Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Anakinra in the Treatment of Gout Flares. Arthritis Rheumatol. 2021 Aug;73(8):1533-1542. DOI: 10.1002/art.41699. PMID 33605029. — 165 pts, 301 flares; anakinra 100 mg/day x 5 days non-inferior to triamcinolone 40 mg single injection.
side_effect_profile_acute: Injection-site reactions: 71% in chronic anakinra (RA dosing). For 5-day flare therapy, rate is lower (~30-40% per study reports) but still common.
Modest infection risk during dosing window; minimal at 5-day acute exposure
side_effect_profile_cumulative_per_year_: Cumulative injection-site burden = real (patient adherence problem at flare onset; injection requires sterile technique and patient self-administration competence)
Anti-drug antibodies: ~3-5% develop neutralizing ADAs over chronic dosing (Saag 2021: ADAs did not affect efficacy at the 5-day flare-therapy regimen)
No characterized long-term toxicity at flare-only dosing pattern
anakinra_sc_qd_for_gout_flare.comment_on_cost: Anakinra retail / wholesale ~$300-500 per 100 mg dose at US specialty-pharmacy pricing. 5 doses/flare x 5 flares/year = ~$7,500-12,500. Substantially cheaper than canakinumab; substantially more expensive than prednisone.

canakinumab_sc_single_dose.regimen: 150 mg SC single dose per flare
side_effect_profile_acute: Modest infection risk; sustained exposure due to ~26-day half-life (vs anakinra ~4h) means risk window per dose is weeks not days
side_effect_profile_cumulative_per_year_: 5 SC injections per year; minimal injection-site burden
Long half-life means immunosuppressive window covers ~3+ months/year cumulatively for a 5-flare patient — non-trivial infection risk over years
No characterized long-term toxicity at flare-only dosing
canakinumab_sc_single_dose.comment_on_cost: Canakinumab list price ~$21,000 per 150 mg dose. The cost is the dominant reason it's reserved for refractory cases.

prednisone_oral_taper.regimen: 30-40 mg/day x 5 days, then taper 5-10 mg every 2-3 days over 7-10 additional days
prednisone_oral_taper.indication: Standard-of-care acute gout flare therapy (oral steroids)
side_effect_profile_acute_per_flare: Glucose elevation (especially in diabetics or pre-diabetics)
Mood disturbances (insomnia, anxiety, mood swings) - very common, ~30-50% report
Sleep disruption - very common at any dose >10 mg/day
Increased appetite, weight gain (transient at acute dose)
side_effect_profile_cumulative_per_year_: Bone density loss: ~6-12 month cumulative steroid exposure starts to show bone effects; 5+ flares/year over multiple years -> measurable osteoporosis risk
Cataract risk: increased with cumulative >2 g lifetime prednisone-equivalent; gout patient with 30-yr disease may cross threshold
Glucose intolerance: cumulative exposure can convert pre-diabetic to diabetic; meaningful risk over years
Adrenal axis suppression: typically requires >2 weeks continuous; 12-15 day per flare with taper is borderline. Cumulative across many flares may cause adrenal-insufficiency physiology
prednisone_oral_taper.comment_on_cost: Generic prednisone is essentially free. Cost is in side-effects, not dollars.

inhaled_mrna_il1ra_repeat_dose_projected.regimen: TBD by comp-036 — likely 1-2 nebulizer sessions per day x 3-5 days at 4-24 mg mRNA per session
side_effect_profile_acute_per_flare_anti: Cough during nebulization (~30-40% per inhaled-mRNA-LNP trial precedent: Translate Bio MRT5005 CF, ReCode RCT2100)
No injection-site reactions (advantage over anakinra/canakinumab SC)
No systemic glucocorticoid effects (advantage over prednisone)
side_effect_profile_cumulative_per_year_: 5 flares x 3-10 nebulizer sessions = 15-50 inhaled mRNA-LNP exposures/year

Anti-PEG antibody buildup: cumulative concern over years of repeat dosing; PEG-related immunogenicity well-characterized for LNP class; mRNA vaccine repeat-dose data (multiple boosters) suggests anti-PEG titers rise but accelerated-clearance has not crossed clinical threshold; for 15-50 inhaled exposures/year this is plausibly bounded but not zero risk

Anti-LNP / anti-mRNA innate immunity (TLR3/RIG-I activation by dsRNA contaminants): mitigated by m1Psi chemistry, but per-session innate activation may be detectable. Long-term repeated exposure risk unknown.
Alveolar irritation: low-grade chronic per-flare exposure unlikely to cause structural lung change at the projected dose count
inhaled_mrna_il1ra_repeat_dose_projected.comment_on_cost: From comp-033 economic analysis. Low-end at COVID-vaccine-scale economics; high-end at specialty-inhaled small-batch pricing. Substantially cheaper than canakinumab across the range; comparable-to-cheaper than anakinra at central economics; only prednisone is meaningfully cheaper in pure-dollar terms.

comparative_framing_per_flare.comment: Per-flare side-effect burden comparison. Subjective patient-experience axes are noted alongside objective clinical axes.
comparative_framing_per_flare.anakinra: 5 SC injections at flare onset (patient must self-inject with active joint pain); injection-site reactions common; minimal systemic side effects; good acute tolerability.
comparative_framing_per_flare.canakinumab: 1 SC injection by clinician at flare visit; injection-site reactions less common; sustained immunosuppressive window of weeks; minimal acute side effects; cost is the limit.
comparative_framing_per_flare.prednisone: Daily oral tablets x 12-15 days; significant mood/sleep/glucose acute side effects; familiar drug; cheap; cumulative-over-years toxicity is the issue.
comparative_framing_per_flare.inhaled_mrna_repeat_dose_projected: 1-2 nebulizer sessions/day x 3-5 days; no injections; transient cough/mucosal irritation; no systemic glucocorticoid burden; cumulative anti-PEG/innate-immunity burden is the long-term unknown.

comparative_framing_cumulative_over_year.comment: For a typical operator-profile patient (Brian, currently affected): 3-6 flares/year, 30+ year disease horizon. Cumulative burden axes that matter at year 5, year 10, year 20.
comparative_framing_cumulative_over_year.anakinra: ~50-150 cumulative SC injections/year/decade; ADAs may rise but typically don't drive efficacy loss at acute-only dosing; injection-site fatigue probable; no characterized severe long-term toxicity.
comparative_framing_cumulative_over_year.canakinumab: ~5-10 SC injections/year over decades; cumulative immunosuppressive windows ~3-12 months/year; long-term infection-risk profile not characterized at this duty cycle; cost is the practical limit.
comparative_framing_cumulative_over_year.prednisone: ~5 flares/year x ~240 mg = ~1,200 mg/year x 30 years = ~36 g cumulative prednisone. Crosses bone-density threshold (typically ~2-5 g cumulative) within 2-4 years; cataract threshold typically ~10 g cumulative; adrenal-axis suppression becomes physiology; this is the durable-toxicity profile the operator is currently accepting.
comparative_framing_cumulative_over_year.inhaled_mrna_repeat_dose_projected: ~15-50 inhaled mRNA-LNP exposures/year x decades = unknown cumulative anti-PEG/innate-immune profile; mRNA vaccine literature (multi-booster cohort tracking) is the closest proxy and suggests acceptable safety at 5+ exposures over 3 years; the inhaled-route and per-session-dose are larger than vaccine boosters, so direct extrapolation has uncertainty; **this is the load-bearing unknown that comp-036 cannot resolve and that wet-lab Phase 1 would need to address.**
